rawText:
Our mission is to ensure that artificial general intelligence—AI systems that are generally smarter than humans—benefits all of humanity.

If AGI is successfully created, this technology could help us elevate humanity by increasing abundance, turbocharging the global economy, and aiding in the discovery of new scientific knowledge that changes the limits of possibility.

AGI has the potential to give everyone incredible new capabilities; we can imagine a world where all of us have access to help with almost any cognitive task, providing a great force multiplier for human ingenuity and creativity.

On the other hand, AGI would also come with serious risk of misuse, drastic accidents, and societal disruption. Because the upside of AGI is so great, we do not believe it is possible or desirable for society to stop its development forever; instead, society and the developers of AGI have to figure out how to get it right.

Although we cannot predict exactly what will happen, and of course our current progress could hit a wall, we can articulate the principles we care about most:

We want AGI to empower humanity to maximally flourish in the universe. We don't expect the future to be an unqualified utopia, but we want to maximize the good and minimize the bad, and for AGI to be an amplifier of humanity.

We want the benefits of, access to, and governance of AGI to be widely and fairly shared.

We want to successfully navigate massive risks. In confronting these risks, we acknowledge that what seems right in theory often plays out more strangely than expected in practice. We believe we have to continuously learn and adapt by deploying less powerful versions of the technology in order to minimize "one shot to get it right" scenarios.

There are several things we think are important to do now to prepare for AGI.

First, as we create successively more powerful systems, we want to deploy them and gain experience with operating them in the real world. We believe this is the best way to carefully steward AGI into existence—a gradual transition to a world with AGI is better than a sudden one. We expect powerful AI to make the rate of progress in the world much faster, and we think it's better to adjust to this incrementally.

A gradual transition gives people, policymakers, and institutions time to understand what's happening, personally experience the benefits and downsides of these systems, adapt our economy, and to put regulation in place. It also allows for society and AI to co-evolve, and for people collectively to figure out what they want while the stakes are relatively low.

sentences.original: Our mission is to ensure that artificial general intelligence—AI systems that are generally smarter than humans—benefits all of humanity.
sentences.translation: 우리의 사명은 인공 일반 지능, 즉 일반적으로 인간보다 더 똑똑한 AI 시스템이 모든 인류에게 혜택을 주도록 보장하는 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: —AI systems that are generally smarter than humans—
grammarElements.label: 삽입구 (대시 사이 동격 설명)

vocabulary.example: The company's mission is to improve education.
We must ensure the safety of all passengers.
Artificial general intelligence remains a major goal in AI research.
This discovery could benefit all of humanity.

expressions.usage: 조직의 핵심 목표를 공식적으로 선언할 때
기술이나 발전의 보편적 혜택을 강조할 때

patterns.pattern: Our mission is to ensure that [subject] [verb] ...
patterns.explanation: 조직의 핵심 목표를 선언하는 공식적인 패턴. "ensure that"으로 보장의 의미를 강조.
examples: Our mission is to ensure that every child has access to education.
Our mission is to ensure that quality remains our top priority.

drillQuestions.question: Our mission is to _____ that AGI benefits all of humanity.
AGI—AI systems that are generally smarter _____ humans—benefits all of humanity.

sentences.notes: 대시(em dash)를 사용한 삽입구가 핵심. "artificial general intelligence"를 정의하기 위해 "—AI systems that are generally smarter than humans—"를 삽입. benefits는 3인칭 단수 현재형으로, 주어가 "artificial general intelligence"(단수)이기 때문.

koreanAlignment.en: —AI systems that are generally smarter than humans—
koreanAlignment.ko: 즉 일반적으로 인간보다 더 똑똑한 AI 시스템이

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 사명 선언(mission statement) — 에세이 첫 문장에서 조직의 핵심 가치를 명확히 선언하며 권위를 확보

sentences.original: If AGI is successfully created, this technology could help us elevate humanity by increasing abundance, turbocharging the global economy, and aiding in the discovery of new scientific knowledge that changes the limits of possibility.
sentences.translation: AGI가 성공적으로 만들어진다면, 이 기술은 풍요를 늘리고, 세계 경제에 활력을 불어넣고, 가능성의 한계를 바꾸는 새로운 과학적 지식의 발견을 도움으로써 인류를 한 단계 높일 수 있을 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: by increasing abundance, turbocharging the global economy, and aiding in the discovery of new scientific knowledge
grammarElements.label: 수단 전치사구 (by + 동명사 3개 병렬)

grammarElements.text: that changes the limits of possibility
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (knowledge를 수식)

vocabulary.example: Education can elevate people out of poverty.
The region has an abundance of natural resources.
New investment could turbocharge economic growth.
Technology aids in the discovery of new medicines.

expressions.usage: help + 목적어 + 동사원형 구조로, 기술의 도움을 표현
기존에 불가능했던 것이 가능해지는 근본적 변화를 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject] could help us [verb] by [verb-ing A], [verb-ing B], and [verb-ing C]
patterns.explanation: 기술이 가져올 혜택을 by + 동명사 병렬 구조로 구체적으로 나열하는 패턴.
examples: AI could help us improve healthcare by diagnosing diseases, personalizing treatment, and reducing costs.
Technology could help us save the environment by monitoring pollution, optimizing energy, and predicting disasters.

drillQuestions.question: This technology could help us elevate humanity _____ increasing abundance.
AGI could aid in the discovery of new scientific knowledge that _____ the limits of possibility.

sentences.notes: by 뒤에 세 개의 동명사(increasing, turbocharging, aiding)가 병렬로 나열. turbocharge는 자동차의 터보차저에서 유래한 비유적 표현으로, "급격히 가속하다"라는 의미. 이 문장은 AGI의 긍정적 비전을 제시하는 핵심 문장.

koreanAlignment.en: this technology could help us elevate humanity
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 기술은 인류를 높일 수 있을 것입니다

koreanAlignment.en: and aiding in the discovery of new scientific knowledge
that changes the limits of possibility

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 열거법(enumeration) — 세 가지 혜택을 병렬로 나열하여 AGI의 잠재적 가치를 극대화

sentences.original: AGI has the potential to give everyone incredible new capabilities; we can imagine a world where all of us have access to help with almost any cognitive task, providing a great force multiplier for human ingenuity and creativity.
sentences.translation: AGI는 모든 사람에게 놀라운 새로운 능력을 줄 잠재력을 가지고 있습니다. 우리 모두가 거의 모든 인지 작업에 대한 도움에 접근할 수 있는 세상을 상상할 수 있으며, 이는 인간의 독창성과 창의성에 대한 엄청난 힘의 배수가 됩니다.

grammarElements.text: where all of us have access to help with almost any cognitive task
grammarElements.label: 관계부사절 (where — world를 수식)

grammarElements.text: providing a great force multiplier for human ingenuity and creativity
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (결과/부가)

vocabulary.example: This project has enormous potential.
Cognitive abilities decline with age.
Technology serves as a force multiplier for small teams.
Human ingenuity has solved many complex problems.

expressions.usage: 미래의 가능성이나 능력을 격식적으로 표현할 때
자원이나 서비스를 이용할 수 있음을 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject] has the potential to give [someone] [something]; we can imagine a world where ...
patterns.explanation: 잠재력을 언급한 후 세미콜론으로 연결하여 구체적 비전을 제시하는 패턴.
examples: Renewable energy has the potential to give every community clean power; we can imagine a world where fossil fuels are obsolete.
The internet has the potential to give everyone access to knowledge; we can imagine a world where education is truly free.

drillQuestions.question: AGI has the _____ to give everyone incredible new capabilities.
We can imagine a world _____ all of us have access to help.

sentences.notes: 세미콜론(;)은 두 독립절을 연결하면서 마침표보다 더 긴밀한 관계를 나타낸다. "force multiplier"는 원래 군사 용어로, 적은 투입으로 큰 효과를 내는 것을 의미. 여기서는 AGI가 인간의 능력을 배가시킨다는 비유.

koreanAlignment.en: AGI has the potential to give
where all of us have access to help
koreanAlignment.ko: 우리 모두가 도움에 접근할 수 있는

sentences.original: On the other hand, AGI would also come with serious risk of misuse, drastic accidents, and societal disruption.
sentences.translation: 반면에, AGI는 심각한 오용의 위험, 극심한 사고, 그리고 사회적 혼란을 수반할 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: serious risk of misuse, drastic accidents, and societal disruption
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (3개 명사구 병렬)

vocabulary.example: The misuse of personal data is a growing concern.
Drastic measures were needed to control the crisis.
The strike caused major disruption to public transport.

expressions.usage: 반대되는 관점이나 측면을 소개할 때
어떤 것에 부수적인 효과나 결과가 있음을 표현

patterns.pattern: On the other hand, [Subject] would also come with [risk A], [risk B], and [risk C]
patterns.explanation: 긍정적 측면을 언급한 뒤, 반대편의 위험을 나열하는 균형 잡힌 논증 패턴.
examples: On the other hand, automation would also come with job losses, inequality, and social tension.
On the other hand, globalization would also come with cultural erosion, economic dependency, and environmental damage.

drillQuestions.question: On the other _____, AGI would come with serious risks.
AGI would also _____ with serious risk of misuse.

sentences.notes: "On the other hand"는 앞 문단의 긍정적 전망과 대비를 이루는 전환 표현. misuse, accidents, disruption의 세 위험 요소를 병렬로 나열하여 AGI의 양면성을 강조.
pronunciationNotes: disruption: /dɪsˈrʌpʃn/ — "디스럽션", startup 생태계에서 자주 쓰이는 단어
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 대조(contrast) — 앞 문단의 낙관적 비전과 대비하여 위험 요소를 제시하는 균형 잡힌 논증

sentences.original: Because the upside of AGI is so great, we do not believe it is possible or desirable for society to stop its development forever; instead, society and the developers of AGI have to figure out how to get it right.
sentences.translation: AGI의 긍정적 면이 매우 크기 때문에, 사회가 AGI의 개발을 영원히 중단하는 것이 가능하거나 바람직하다고 생각하지 않습니다. 대신, 사회와 AGI 개발자들은 이것을 올바르게 해내는 방법을 알아내야 합니다.

grammarElements.text: Because the upside of AGI is so great
grammarElements.label: 이유 종속절 (because)

grammarElements.text: it is possible or desirable for society to stop its development forever
grammarElements.label: that절 (생략된 접속사 that)

grammarElements.text: society and the developers of AGI
grammarElements.label: 주어 (복합)

vocabulary.example: The upside of remote work is flexibility.
A peaceful resolution is the most desirable outcome.
We need to figure out a solution to this problem.

expressions.usage: 실수 없이 올바른 결과를 달성하는 것을 표현

expressions.phrase: do not believe it is possible or desirable
expressions.usage: 이중 부정의 뉘앙스로 강한 입장을 표현

patterns.pattern: Because [X] is so great, we do not believe it is [adjective A] or [adjective B] to [verb]; instead, [subject] have to figure out how to [verb]
patterns.explanation: 한 가지 입장을 부정한 뒤, "instead"로 대안을 제시하는 논증 패턴.
examples: Because the potential is so great, we do not believe it is wise or practical to give up; instead, we have to figure out how to succeed.

drillQuestions.question: We do not believe it is possible or _____ to stop AGI development.
Society has to figure _____ how to get it right.

sentences.notes: "for society to stop"에서 for + 명사 + to부정사는 의미상 주어 구문. "get it right"은 구어적이지만 여기서는 "인류의 미래를 올바르게 설계하다"라는 무거운 의미를 담고 있어 격식적 글에서의 전략적 구어 사용.

koreanAlignment.en: Because the upside of AGI is so great
koreanAlignment.ko: AGI의 긍정적 면이 매우 크기 때문에

koreanAlignment.en: it is possible or desirable for society
instead, society and the developers of AGI
have to figure out how to get it right

sentences.original: Although we cannot predict exactly what will happen, and of course our current progress could hit a wall, we can articulate the principles we care about most:
sentences.translation: 비록 무슨 일이 일어날지 정확히 예측할 수 없고, 물론 현재의 진전이 벽에 부딪힐 수도 있지만, 우리가 가장 중요하게 여기는 원칙들을 명확히 표현할 수 있습니다:

grammarElements.text: Although we cannot predict exactly what will happen
grammarElements.label: 양보절 (although)

grammarElements.text: and of course our current progress could hit a wall
grammarElements.label: 등위 양보절 (and)

grammarElements.text: the principles we care about most
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (관계대명사 목적격 생략)

vocabulary.example: It is difficult to predict the weather accurately.
She articulated her vision for the company clearly.
Our research hit a wall after two years.

expressions.usage: 양보의 의미를 겹쳐 사용하여 겸손함과 현실 인식을 동시에 표현

expressions.phrase: the principles we care about most
expressions.usage: 핵심 가치를 소개하기 전의 도입 표현

patterns.pattern: Although we cannot [verb] exactly ..., we can [verb] the principles we care about most:
patterns.explanation: 불확실성을 인정하면서도 핵심 가치를 명확히 밝히는 양보 + 주절 패턴. 콜론(:)으로 이어지는 내용을 예고.
examples: Although we cannot guarantee the result, we can articulate the values we stand for:
Although we cannot control every variable, we can define the goals we aim for:

drillQuestions.question: _____ we cannot predict exactly what will happen, we can articulate our principles.
Our current progress could _____ a wall.

sentences.notes: articulate는 형용사("명확한")와 동사("명확히 표현하다") 두 가지 용법이 있다. 여기서는 동사. "hit a wall"은 은유적 표현으로, AI 연구의 진전이 멈출 수 있다는 겸손한 인정. 문장 끝의 콜론(:)은 이어지는 원칙 목록을 예고.

koreanAlignment.en: Although we cannot predict exactly what will happen
koreanAlignment.ko: 비록 무슨 일이 일어날지 정확히 예측할 수 없고

koreanAlignment.en: and of course our current progress could hit a wall
koreanAlignment.ko: 물론 현재의 진전이 벽에 부딪힐 수도 있지만

koreanAlignment.en: the principles we care about most

pronunciationNotes: principles: /ˈprɪnsɪplz/ — "프린서플즈", principal(교장)과 발음 동일하나 의미 다름
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 양보 후 주장(concession-assertion) — 불확실성을 솔직히 인정함으로써 이어지는 원칙의 신뢰도를 높임

sentences.original: We want AGI to empower humanity to maximally flourish in the universe.
sentences.translation: 우리는 AGI가 인류가 우주에서 최대한 번영하도록 힘을 실어주기를 원합니다.

grammarElements.text: to maximally flourish in the universe
grammarElements.label: 중첩 to부정사 (humanity의 목적격 보어)

vocabulary.example: Education empowers people to make better decisions.
The arts flourished during the Renaissance.
We want to maximally utilize our resources.

expressions.phrase: we want X to empower Y to Z
expressions.meaning: X가 Y에게 Z할 힘을 주기를 원한다
expressions.usage: 기술이 사람에게 권한을 부여하는 비전을 표현

patterns.pattern: We want [X] to empower [Y] to [verb] ...
patterns.explanation: want + 목적어 + to부정사가 두 번 중첩되는 구조. 기술 → 인류 → 번영의 인과관계를 한 문장에 담음.
examples: We want technology to empower students to learn independently.
We want AI to empower doctors to diagnose more accurately.

drillQuestions.question: We want AGI to _____ humanity to flourish.
We want humanity to maximally _____ in the universe.

sentences.notes: 중첩된 to부정사 구조에 주목: want AGI [to empower] humanity [to flourish]. "maximally flourish in the universe"는 의도적으로 거대한 비전을 표현. flourish는 flower와 같은 라틴어 어원(florere)에서 유래.

sentences.original: We don't expect the future to be an unqualified utopia, but we want to maximize the good and minimize the bad, and for AGI to be an amplifier of humanity.
sentences.translation: 우리는 미래가 무조건적인 유토피아가 되리라 기대하지 않지만, 좋은 것을 극대화하고 나쁜 것을 최소화하며, AGI가 인류의 증폭기가 되기를 원합니다.

grammarElements.text: the future to be an unqualified utopia
grammarElements.label: 목적어 + 목적격 보어 (expect + O + to V)

grammarElements.text: but we want to maximize the good and minimize the bad
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (but)

grammarElements.text: and for AGI to be an amplifier of humanity
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (for + 명사 + to부정사)

vocabulary.example: The project was an unqualified success.
A perfect society is a utopia that many have dreamed of.
Music sounds better through an amplifier.
We need to maximize efficiency and minimize waste.

expressions.usage: 비현실적 기대를 부정하면서 현실적 접근을 보일 때

expressions.phrase: maximize the good and minimize the bad
expressions.meaning: 좋은 것을 극대화하고 나쁜 것을 최소화하다
expressions.usage: 균형 잡힌 목표를 표현하는 대구 표현

patterns.pattern: We don't expect [X] to be [ideal], but we want to [verb A] and [verb B]
patterns.explanation: 비현실적 기대를 부정한 뒤, 현실적이고 구체적인 목표를 제시하는 패턴.
examples: We don't expect the system to be perfect, but we want to improve reliability and reduce errors.
We don't expect instant results, but we want to build momentum and create lasting change.

drillQuestions.question: We don't expect the future to be an _____ utopia.
We want AGI to be an _____ of humanity.

sentences.notes: "unqualified"는 보통 "자격이 없는"이라는 의미로 알려져 있지만, 여기서는 "조건 없는, 완전한"이라는 의미. "amplifier of humanity"는 AGI를 인간성을 증폭시키는 도구로 정의하는 핵심 은유. maximize/minimize의 대구가 균형 잡힌 접근을 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: but we want to maximize the good
and for AGI to be an amplifier of humanity

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 대구법(antithesis) — maximize/minimize 대비와 good/bad 대비가 균형 잡힌 비전을 전달

sentences.original: We want the benefits of, access to, and governance of AGI to be widely and fairly shared.
sentences.translation: 우리는 AGI의 혜택, 접근성, 그리고 거버넌스가 널리 그리고 공정하게 공유되기를 원합니다.

grammarElements.text: the benefits of, access to, and governance of AGI
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (3개 명사구 병렬 — 전치사 공유 구조)

grammarElements.text: to be widely and fairly shared
grammarElements.label: 목적격 보어 (to be + 수동태)

vocabulary.example: Good governance is essential for economic development.
The news was widely reported.
Resources should be distributed fairly.

expressions.phrase: the benefits of, access to, and governance of
expressions.usage: 세 가지 측면(혜택/접근/관리)을 병렬로 나열하되 전치사가 각각 다른 고급 구조
분배의 범위(widely)와 공정성(fairly) 두 가지 가치를 동시에 강조

patterns.pattern: We want the [noun A] of, [noun B] to, and [noun C] of [X] to be [adverb] [past participle]
patterns.explanation: 서로 다른 전치사를 가진 명사구를 병렬로 나열하는 고급 구조. 형식적 글쓰기에서 자주 사용.
examples: We want the production of, investment in, and distribution of clean energy to be equitably managed.

drillQuestions.question: We want the benefits of, access _____, and governance of AGI to be shared.
AGI should be widely and _____ shared.

sentences.notes: 이 문장의 핵심은 "benefits of, access to, and governance of" 구조. 세 명사(benefits, access, governance)가 각각 다른 전치사(of, to, of)를 취하면서 AGI라는 하나의 목적어를 공유. 영어 작문에서 매우 세련된 병렬 구조.

koreanAlignment.en: to be widely and fairly shared

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 병렬 구조(parallelism) — 서로 다른 전치사를 가진 세 명사구를 우아하게 병렬 처리하여 핵심 가치를 압축 전달

sentences.original: We want to successfully navigate massive risks.
sentences.translation: 우리는 거대한 위험을 성공적으로 헤쳐나가기를 원합니다.

vocabulary.example: The company navigated through the financial crisis.
The project requires a massive investment.

expressions.usage: 항해 은유를 사용하여 어려운 상황을 통과하는 것을 표현

patterns.pattern: We want to successfully [verb] [adjective] [noun]
patterns.explanation: 목표를 간결하게 선언하는 패턴. 부사(successfully)가 동사 앞에 위치하여 방법을 강조.
examples: We want to successfully address growing inequality.
We want to successfully manage complex transitions.

drillQuestions.question: We want to successfully _____ massive risks.

sentences.notes: navigate는 원래 "항해하다"라는 의미에서 비유적으로 "어려운 상황을 헤쳐나가다"로 확장. 짧고 강력한 선언문으로, 앞의 긴 문장들과 대비하여 임팩트를 줌.

sentences.original: In confronting these risks, we acknowledge that what seems right in theory often plays out more strangely than expected in practice.
sentences.translation: 이러한 위험에 맞서면서, 우리는 이론적으로 옳아 보이는 것이 실제로는 종종 예상보다 더 이상하게 전개된다는 것을 인정합니다.

grammarElements.text: often plays out more strangely than expected
grammarElements.label: that절의 동사 (비교급)

vocabulary.example: We must confront the challenges ahead.
The company acknowledged its mistake.
We will see how events play out.

expressions.usage: 이론과 실제의 괴리를 표현하는 대비 쌍
예상과 현실의 차이를 강조

patterns.pattern: In confronting [X], we acknowledge that what seems [adjective] in theory often [verb] more [adverb] than expected in practice.
patterns.explanation: 이론과 실제의 괴리를 인정하는 겸손한 논증 패턴. 학술적/정책적 글에서 자주 사용.
examples: In confronting climate change, we acknowledge that what seems effective in theory often works out more differently than expected in practice.

drillQuestions.question: What seems right in theory often plays _____ differently in practice.
In _____ these risks, we acknowledge the challenges.

sentences.notes: "what seems right in theory often plays out more strangely than expected in practice"는 이론과 실제의 괴리에 대한 깊은 인식을 보여주는 문장. "play out"은 연극 용어에서 유래하여 "사건이 전개되다"라는 의미.

koreanAlignment.en: often plays out more strangely than expected
koreanAlignment.ko: 종종 예상보다 더 이상하게 전개된다는 것을

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 이론-실제 대비(theory-practice contrast) — 학문적 겸손을 보이면서 실용주의적 접근의 필요성을 정당화

sentences.original: We believe we have to continuously learn and adapt by deploying less powerful versions of the technology in order to minimize "one shot to get it right" scenarios.
sentences.translation: 우리는 "한 번에 제대로 해야 하는" 시나리오를 최소화하기 위해 덜 강력한 버전의 기술을 배포하면서 지속적으로 배우고 적응해야 한다고 믿습니다.

grammarElements.text: we have to continuously learn and adapt
grammarElements.label: that절 (생략된 that)

grammarElements.text: by deploying less powerful versions of the technology
grammarElements.label: 수단 전치사구 (by + 동명사)

grammarElements.text: "one shot to get it right" scenarios
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (인용구가 형용사 역할)

vocabulary.example: Technology is continuously evolving.
The company deployed the new software worldwide.
We need to prepare for the worst-case scenario.

expressions.phrase: one shot to get it right
expressions.meaning: 한 번에 제대로 해야 하는 기회
expressions.usage: 실패가 허용되지 않는 고위험 상황을 표현
목적을 명확히 표현하는 격식적 구문

patterns.pattern: We believe we have to [verb] by [verb-ing] in order to [verb]
patterns.explanation: 신념(believe) → 의무(have to) → 수단(by) → 목적(in order to)의 논리적 연쇄 구조.
examples: We believe we have to innovate by investing in R&D in order to stay competitive.
We believe we have to reduce emissions by transitioning to clean energy in order to combat climate change.

drillQuestions.question: We have to continuously learn and adapt _____ deploying less powerful versions.
We want to minimize "one shot to get it _____" scenarios.

sentences.notes: "one shot to get it right"이 따옴표 안에 있는 것은 이 구절 자체가 하나의 개념으로 사용되기 때문. 점진적 배포(gradual deployment)가 AI 안전의 핵심 전략임을 논증하는 문장. deploy는 원래 군사 용어로 "부대를 배치하다"에서 유래.

koreanAlignment.en: we have to continuously learn and adapt
by deploying less powerful versions of the technology
"one shot to get it right" scenarios

sentences.original: There are several things we think are important to do now to prepare for AGI.
sentences.translation: AGI에 대비하기 위해 지금 해야 할 중요한 일들이 몇 가지 있다고 생각합니다.

vocabulary.example: We need to prepare for the challenges ahead.

expressions.phrase: there are several things we think are important
expressions.meaning: 우리가 중요하다고 생각하는 몇 가지 것들이 있다
expressions.usage: 주요 포인트를 소개하기 전 도입문으로 사용

patterns.pattern: There are several things we think are important to [verb] to [verb]
patterns.explanation: 여러 포인트를 나열하기 전 도입하는 패턴. 관계대명사 생략과 삽입절(we think)이 특징.
examples: There are several things we think are important to consider to ensure success.
There are several factors we think are important to address to improve education.

drillQuestions.question: There are several things we think _____ important to do now.
We need to prepare _____ AGI.

sentences.notes: "things (that) we think are important"에서 관계대명사 that이 생략되고, "we think"이 삽입절로 들어감. 구조는 things [that] are important인데, 중간에 we think가 삽입. 이 문장은 에세이의 구조적 전환점으로, "The short term" 섹션의 시작.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 전환(transition) — 추상적 원칙에서 구체적 실행 계획으로 넘어가는 구조적 전환점

sentences.original: First, as we create successively more powerful systems, we want to deploy them and gain experience with operating them in the real world.
sentences.translation: 첫째, 점차적으로 더 강력한 시스템을 만들어 감에 따라, 우리는 그것들을 배포하고 실제 세계에서 운영하는 경험을 쌓기를 원합니다.

grammarElements.text: as we create successively more powerful systems
grammarElements.label: 종속절 (시간/조건 — as)

grammarElements.text: want to deploy them and gain experience
grammarElements.label: 동사 (want to + 두 동사 병렬)

grammarElements.text: with operating them in the real world
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (경험의 대상)

vocabulary.example: Each generation of chips became successively more powerful.
You can gain experience by working on real projects.
The system is easy to operate.

expressions.phrase: as we create successively more powerful systems
expressions.meaning: 점차 더 강력한 시스템을 만들어 감에 따라
expressions.usage: as를 사용하여 동시 진행되는 과정을 표현
실무 경험의 중요성을 강조

patterns.pattern: First, as we [verb A], we want to [verb B] and [verb C]
patterns.explanation: "First"로 첫 번째 포인트를 도입하고, as절로 조건을 설정한 뒤 목표를 제시하는 패턴.
examples: First, as we expand our team, we want to maintain quality and strengthen culture.
First, as we scale production, we want to reduce costs and improve efficiency.

drillQuestions.question: As we create _____ more powerful systems, we want to deploy them.
We want to gain experience _____ operating them in the real world.

sentences.notes: successively는 "연속적으로 점차"라는 의미로, successive(연속적인)의 부사형. "gain experience with operating"에서 with 뒤에 동명사가 오는 구조에 주목. 이 문장은 OpenAI의 점진적 배포 전략의 핵심.

koreanAlignment.en: as we create successively more powerful systems
koreanAlignment.ko: 점차 더 강력한 시스템을 만들어 감에 따라

koreanAlignment.en: with operating them in the real world

sentences.original: We believe this is the best way to carefully steward AGI into existence—a gradual transition to a world with AGI is better than a sudden one.
sentences.translation: 우리는 이것이 AGI를 신중하게 존재로 이끄는 최선의 방법이라고 믿습니다. AGI가 있는 세계로의 점진적 전환이 갑작스러운 것보다 낫습니다.

grammarElements.text: to carefully steward AGI into existence
grammarElements.label: to부정사 (way를 수식)

grammarElements.text: —a gradual transition to a world with AGI is better than a sudden one
grammarElements.label: 대시 뒤 부연 설명

vocabulary.example: Leaders must steward resources responsibly.
The recovery was slow and gradual.
The transition to renewable energy will take decades.

expressions.usage: 새로운 것을 책임감 있게 세상에 내놓는 과정을 표현. steward를 동사로 사용하는 독특한 용법

expressions.phrase: a gradual transition is better than a sudden one
expressions.usage: 변화의 속도에 대한 입장을 비교급으로 표현

patterns.pattern: We believe this is the best way to [verb]—[explanation]
patterns.explanation: 대시를 사용하여 핵심 주장을 부연 설명하는 패턴.
examples: We believe this is the best way to build trust—transparency leads to stronger relationships.
We believe this is the best way to innovate—small experiments lead to big breakthroughs.

drillQuestions.question: We believe this is the best way to carefully _____ AGI into existence.
A gradual transition is better _____ a sudden one.

sentences.notes: steward는 보통 명사("관리인, 승무원")로 알려져 있지만, 여기서는 동사로 사용. "steward AGI into existence"는 마치 새 생명을 돌보듯 AI를 세상에 내놓겠다는 책임감을 표현. 대시(—) 뒤의 문장이 핵심 논지를 요약.

koreanAlignment.en: to carefully steward AGI into existence
a gradual transition to a world with AGI
is better than a sudden one

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 은유(metaphor) — "steward into existence"는 AGI를 돌봄이 필요한 존재로 비유하여 책임감을 강조

sentences.original: We expect powerful AI to make the rate of progress in the world much faster, and we think it's better to adjust to this incrementally.
sentences.translation: 우리는 강력한 AI가 세계의 발전 속도를 훨씬 빠르게 만들 것으로 예상하며, 이에 점진적으로 적응하는 것이 더 낫다고 생각합니다.

grammarElements.text: to make the rate of progress in the world much faster
grammarElements.label: 목적격 보어 (expect + O + to V)

vocabulary.example: The rate of progress in AI has been remarkable.
It takes time to adjust to a new environment.
The software is updated incrementally.

expressions.phrase: make the rate of progress much faster
expressions.usage: make + 목적어 + 형용사 비교급 구조로 변화를 표현
급격한 변화가 아닌 단계적 적응을 주장

patterns.pattern: We expect [X] to make [Y] [comparative], and we think it's better to [verb] [adverb]
patterns.explanation: 예측(expect)과 의견(think)을 등위절로 연결하여 입장을 밝히는 패턴.
examples: We expect automation to make production much cheaper, and we think it's better to prepare for this gradually.
We expect remote work to make collaboration more global, and we think it's better to adapt to this proactively.

drillQuestions.question: We expect powerful AI _____ make the rate of progress faster.
We think it's better to adjust to this _____.

sentences.notes: "expect + 목적어 + to부정사"와 "think it's better + to부정사" 두 가지 구문이 and로 연결. incrementally는 increment(증가분)에서 파생된 부사로, 컴퓨터 과학에서 자주 쓰이는 용어가 일반 담론으로 확장.

koreanAlignment.en: to make the rate of progress in the world much faster
koreanAlignment.ko: 세계의 발전 속도를 훨씬 빠르게 만들 것으로 예상하며

sentences.original: A gradual transition gives people, policymakers, and institutions time to understand what's happening, personally experience the benefits and downsides of these systems, adapt our economy, and to put regulation in place.
sentences.translation: 점진적 전환은 사람들, 정책 입안자들, 그리고 기관들에게 무슨 일이 일어나고 있는지 이해하고, 이러한 시스템의 장단점을 직접 경험하고, 경제를 적응시키고, 규제를 마련할 시간을 줍니다.

grammarElements.text: to understand what's happening, personally experience the benefits and downsides, adapt our economy, and to put regulation in place
grammarElements.label: to부정사구 (4개 동사 병렬 — time을 수식)

vocabulary.example: Policymakers need to consider the long-term effects.
The downside of the plan is its high cost.
New regulations were introduced to protect consumers.

expressions.usage: 법적/제도적 틀을 수립하는 것을 표현
추상적이 아닌 실제 체험을 강조

patterns.pattern: [Subject] gives [people A, B, and C] time to [verb 1], [verb 2], [verb 3], and to [verb 4]
patterns.explanation: 4형식(give + 간접목적어 + 직접목적어) 뒤에 to부정사가 여러 동사를 병렬로 나열하는 복합 구조.
examples: Remote work gives employees, managers, and teams time to adapt workflows, redesign spaces, update tools, and to rethink collaboration.

drillQuestions.question: A gradual transition gives people time to understand what _____ happening.
We need to put regulation _____ place.

sentences.notes: 네 개의 to부정사(to understand, experience, adapt, to put)가 time을 수식. 마지막 to put에만 다시 to가 나타나는 것은 병렬 구조가 길어질 때 마지막 항목에 to를 반복하여 구조를 명확히 하는 작문 기법.

koreanAlignment.en: personally experience the benefits and downsides
and to put regulation in place

sentences.original: It also allows for society and AI to co-evolve, and for people collectively to figure out what they want while the stakes are relatively low.
sentences.translation: 이는 또한 사회와 AI가 함께 진화하고, 위험 부담이 비교적 낮은 동안 사람들이 집단적으로 무엇을 원하는지 알아낼 수 있게 해줍니다.

grammarElements.text: and for people collectively to figure out what they want
grammarElements.label: 병렬 for + 명사 + to부정사

grammarElements.text: while the stakes are relatively low
grammarElements.label: 종속절 (시간/조건 — while)

vocabulary.example: Languages and cultures co-evolve over centuries.
We must collectively address climate change.
The stakes are very high in this negotiation.

expressions.usage: allow와 달리 allow for는 여지를 두거나 가능성을 열어두는 뉘앙스

expressions.phrase: while the stakes are relatively low
expressions.usage: 지금 행동해야 하는 시기적 이유를 표현

patterns.pattern: It allows for [A] to [verb 1], and for [B] to [verb 2] while [condition]
patterns.explanation: allow for + 두 개의 for 구문이 병렬로 연결되고, while절로 조건을 부가하는 복합 구조.
examples: It allows for teams to experiment, and for leaders to learn while the risks are manageable.
It allows for students to make mistakes, and for teachers to adjust while the pressure is low.

drillQuestions.question: It allows for society and AI to _____.
People can figure out what they want while the _____ are relatively low.

sentences.notes: co-evolve는 생물학의 공진화(coevolution) 개념에서 가져온 용어로, 사회와 AI가 서로 영향을 주며 함께 발전한다는 의미. "while the stakes are relatively low"는 지금이 실험하기 좋은 시기라는 시급성을 내포.

koreanAlignment.en: to figure out what they want
while the stakes are relatively low

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 공진화 은유(coevolution metaphor) — 생물학 개념을 차용하여 사회와 AI의 상호작용적 발전을 표현

sentences.original: We currently believe the best way to successfully navigate AI deployment challenges is with a tight feedback loop of rapid learning and careful iteration.
sentences.translation: 우리는 현재 AI 배포 과제를 성공적으로 헤쳐나가는 최선의 방법은 빠른 학습과 신중한 반복의 긴밀한 피드백 루프를 통하는 것이라고 믿습니다.

grammarElements.text: the best way to successfully navigate AI deployment challenges
grammarElements.label: that절의 주어 (that 생략)

grammarElements.text: with a tight feedback loop of rapid learning and careful iteration
grammarElements.label: 보어 (전치사구)

vocabulary.example: Customer feedback loops help improve products.
Each iteration of the design was better than the last.
The deployment of new technology requires careful planning.

expressions.usage: 입력과 출력 사이의 빠른 순환을 표현. 스타트업/기술 문화에서 자주 사용
속도(rapid)와 신중함(careful)의 균형을 동시에 강조

patterns.pattern: We believe the best way to [verb] [noun] is with [a method/approach]
patterns.explanation: 최선의 방법을 "is with"로 제시하는 패턴. 추상적 과제에 구체적 해결 방법을 연결.
examples: We believe the best way to build trust is with consistent transparency.
We believe the best way to manage risk is with diversified strategies.

drillQuestions.question: The best way to navigate challenges is with a tight _____ loop.
We need rapid learning and careful _____.

sentences.notes: "tight feedback loop"은 시스템 이론과 소프트웨어 개발에서 온 용어. "rapid learning and careful iteration"에서 rapid(빠른)과 careful(신중한)의 의도적 대비에 주목. 속도와 신중함의 균형이 핵심 메시지.

koreanAlignment.en: the best way to successfully navigate AI deployment challenges
koreanAlignment.ko: AI 배포 과제를 성공적으로 헤쳐나가는 최선의 방법은

koreanAlignment.en: is with a tight feedback loop
of rapid learning and careful iteration

sentences.original: Generally speaking, we think more usage of AI in the world will lead to good, and want to promote it.
sentences.translation: 일반적으로 말해서, 우리는 세계에서 AI의 더 많은 사용이 좋은 결과로 이어질 것이라고 생각하며, 이를 촉진하고 싶습니다.

grammarElements.text: more usage of AI in the world will lead to good
grammarElements.label: that절 (생략된 that)

vocabulary.example: Generally speaking, the project was a success.
Data usage has increased dramatically.
The government wants to promote renewable energy.
Hard work leads to success.

expressions.usage: 전반적인 입장을 밝히면서 예외가 있을 수 있음을 암시하는 담화표지
good을 명사처럼 사용하여 "좋은 것, 선"이라는 추상적 의미를 표현

patterns.pattern: Generally speaking, we think [X] will lead to [Y], and want to [verb] it
patterns.explanation: 일반적 입장을 밝힌 뒤 행동 의지를 덧붙이는 패턴.
examples: Generally speaking, we think open access will lead to innovation, and want to support it.
Generally speaking, we think education reform will lead to progress, and want to advocate for it.

drillQuestions.question: Generally _____, we think more AI usage will be good.
More usage of AI will lead _____ good.

sentences.notes: "lead to good"에서 good은 형용사가 아닌 명사로 사용("좋은 것, 선"). "generally speaking"은 뒤에 오는 입장이 절대적이 아닌 일반적 경향임을 표시하는 헤징(hedging) 표현.

koreanAlignment.en: we think more usage of AI in the world
